reasoning: The Lean signature captures the informal statement faithfully. The hypothesis `hm : m = p.degree` (with the standard coercion ℕ → WithBot ℕ) asserts that p has degree m, matching "p has degree m". The left side of the iff, `card (p.rootSet ℂ) = m`, counts distinct complex roots (rootSet is a Set, so no multiplicity), matching "p has m distinct roots". The right side, `Disjoint (p.rootSet ℂ) ((derivative p).rootSet ℂ)`, says the sets of roots of p and p' share no elements, matching "p and p' have no roots in common". The `hp : p ≠ 0` hypothesis is redundant given the degree equation (since a natural number cannot equal ⊥) but does not alter the mathematical content.
